questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All books are pens. All pens are boxes. Conclusions: I. All books are boxes. II. All pens are books.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All readers are teachers. All writers are teachers. Conclusions: I. Some readers are writers. II. Some writers are readers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All dogs are fish. Some rats are fish. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are lizards. II. Some lizards are dogs.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All lawyers are cheats. Some women are lawyers. Conclusions: I. Some women are cheats. II. All cheats are women.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All plants are trees. No tree is green. Conclusions: I. Some plants are green. II. Those plants which are not trees are green.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All hill-stations have a sunset point. X is a hill-station. Conclusions: I. X has a sunset point. II. Places other than hill-stations do not have sunset point.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some girls are flowers. Some flowers are books. Conclusions: I. Some girls are books. II. No book is a girl.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some parrots are crows. No crow is green. Conclusions: I. No parrot is green. II. No crow is white.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All clerks are lazy. Some men are clerks. Conclusions: I. All men are men. II. Some men are lazy.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All goats are wolves. Some wolves are tigers. Conclusions: I. Some goats are tigers. II. Tigers which are wolves are not goats.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some pots are belts. No belt is white. Conclusions: I. Some pots are white. II. Some pots are not white.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some aeroplanes are living beings. No living being can fly without wings. Conclusions: I. Some aeroplanes are ghosts. II. Some aeroplanes are not ghosts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some papers are needles. All needles are sharp. Conclusions: I. All papers are sharp. II. All needles are papers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All lamps are cakes. All lamps are bags. Conclusions: I. No lamp is a bag. II. Some cakes are bags.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some soldiers are famous. Some soldiers are intelligent. Conclusions: I. Some famous soldiers are either famous or intelligent. II. Some soldiers are neither famous nor intelligent.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All radios are pencils. Some pencils are files. Conclusions: I. No radio is a file. II. Some files are radios.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some machines are racks. All wheels are machines. Conclusions: I. Some wheels are racks. II. Some racks are wheels.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All roads are poles. No pole is a house. Conclusions: I. Some roads are houses. II. Some houses are poles.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some men are educated. Educated persons prefer small families. Conclusions: I. All small families are educated. II. Some men prefer small families.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All players are tall. Sohan is tall. Conclusions: I. Sohan is a player. II. Sohan is not a player.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All students are boys. No boy is dull. Conclusions: I. No student is dull. II. There are no girls in the class.
